summary: Civil liberties organizations filed suit challenging Arkansas age verification law under the First Amendment.
District court entered a preliminary injunction blocking enforcement pending litigation.

case_name: Free Speech Coalition, Inc. v. Paxton
court: Supreme Court of the United States
event_summary: SCOTUS held HB 1181 survives intermediate scrutiny.

court: Supreme Court of the United States
event_summary: Application to vacate stay denied.

case_name: California minors social-media regulation litigation cluster
court: State and federal courts (multi-stage)
event_summary: Active litigation posture noted for SB 976 implementation and constitutional review context.

court: Federal courts (multiple stages)
event_summary: Active constitutional litigation posture; refresh from docket required each update cycle.

court: Supreme Court of the United States
event_summary: SCOTUS unanimously struck down the CDA's indecency provisions as violating the First Amendment; held the internet is entitled to the highest level of First Amendment protection (strict scrutiny); Section 230 survived.

court: U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania
event_summary: District court entered preliminary injunction blocking COPA enforcement; law never took effect.

court: Supreme Court of the United States
event_summary: SCOTUS affirmed injunction and remanded; held COPA likely violates First Amendment because less restrictive alternatives (e.g., filtering software) could be as effective.

court: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit
event_summary: Third Circuit affirmed permanent injunction against COPA; SCOTUS denied cert Jan 21, 2009, finally ending the law.

court: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California
event_summary: District court entered preliminary injunction blocking CAADCA before its Jul 1, 2024 effective date; found NetChoice likely to succeed on First Amendment grounds.

court: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit
event_summary: 9th Circuit partially vacated the district court's preliminary injunction; remanded for more detailed analysis on some provisions while others remained enjoined.

case_name: NetChoice v. Bonta (9th Cir.) – second ruling
court: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit
event_summary: 9th Circuit second ruling: 5 of 6 CAADCA provisions remain enjoined; only one provision survived as consistent with the First Amendment.

court: U.S. District Court for the District of Utah
event_summary: Chief Judge Shelby entered preliminary injunction against Utah SB 152; rendered moot shortly after when SB 152 was repealed and replaced by SB 194.

court: U.S. District Court for the District of Utah
event_summary: Chief Judge Robert J. Shelby entered preliminary injunction blocking Utah SB 194 before its Oct 1, 2024 effective date; enforcement halted pending continued litigation.

court: U.S. District Court for the Western District of Arkansas
event_summary: Judge Timothy L. Brooks entered preliminary injunction blocking Arkansas SB 396 before its Sep 1, 2023 effective date on First Amendment grounds.

case_name: NetChoice LLC v. Griffin, 2025 WL 978607 (W.D. Ark.)
court: U.S. District Court for the Western District of Arkansas
event_summary: First permanent injunction obtained by NetChoice against any state social media law; court held SB 396 (Act 689) unconstitutional under First Amendment; Arkansas appealing.

court: U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Mississippi
event_summary: Judge Halil Suleyman Ozerden entered preliminary injunction blocking MS HB 1126 on the same day it was meant to take effect; enforcement halted pending litigation.

court: U.S. District Court for the Middle District of Louisiana
event_summary: NetChoice filed constitutional challenge against Louisiana SOCIAL Act (SB 162) before its Jul 1, 2025 effective date; litigation ongoing.

court: U.S. District Court for the District of Maryland
event_summary: NetChoice filed constitutional challenge against Maryland Kids Code (HB 603); preliminary injunction motion pending as of Apr 2026.

court: U.S. District Court for the District of Colorado
event_summary: Court stayed implementation of Colorado Social Media Warning Label Law (HB 24-1136) in November 2025 before its Jan 1, 2026 effective date.

court: U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Ohio
event_summary: Chief Judge Algenon L. Marbley entered TRO blocking Ohio HB 33 Social Media Parental Notification Act before its Jan 15, 2024 effective date; subsequently confirmed as preliminary injunction.

case_name: NetChoice LLC v. Yost, 2025 WL 1137485 (S.D. Ohio)
court: U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Ohio
event_summary: Permanent injunction entered against Ohio HB 33 Social Media Parental Notification Act; Ohio appealed to 6th Circuit May 12, 2025; appeal pending.

court: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California
event_summary: Computer & Communications Industry Association filed constitutional challenge against California Digital Age Assurance Act (AB 1043) days after signing on Oct 13, 2025; preliminary injunction motion status pending as of Apr 2026.

case_name: SEAT v. Paxton; CCIA & NetChoice v. Paxton (W.D. Tex.)
court: U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas
event_summary: Preliminary injunctions granted in two consolidated cases blocking Texas App Store Accountability Act (SB 2420) before its Jan 1, 2026 effective date; court found AV and parental consent requirements likely fail strict scrutiny as content-based restrictions not narrowly tailored; cases on appeal.

court: U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas
event_summary: Judge Pitman entered preliminary injunction against Texas SCOPE Act content monitoring/filtering provisions; app on appeal to 5th Circuit (No. 24-50721).

court: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Texas
event_summary: Broader preliminary injunction entered blocking content monitoring, filtering, advertising, AND age verification requirements of Texas SCOPE Act (HB 18); on appeal to 5th Circuit (No. 25-50096).

court: U.S. District Court for the Middle District of Tennessee
event_summary: NetChoice filed constitutional challenge against Tennessee Protecting Children from Social Media Act (HB 1891); no preliminary injunction obtained as of Apr 2026 (contrast with most other state social media laws); case management delayed pending U.S. v. Skrmetti SCOTUS ruling.

case_name: NetChoice LLC v. Griffin (W.D. Ark.) – extended to Act 900
court: U.S. District Court for the Western District of Arkansas
event_summary: Permanent injunction against Act 689 (SB 396) extends to most provisions of Act 900 (SB 611); curfew/notification provisions effective Apr 21, 2026 may be outside scope of existing injunction; Arkansas appealing.

case_name: NetChoice v. Bonta (N.D. Cal., Judge Davila) – SB 976
court: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California
event_summary: Judge Davila entered preliminary injunction blocking time-of-day notification restrictions and reporting requirements of CA SB 976; age assurance and addictive-feed restrictions remain in force; full stay briefly granted Jan 2, 2025 then partially lifted; 9th Circuit appeal ongoing.
